raw_response:
## Overall Assessment

Apex demonstrates a functioning maintenance program for Q4 2025: recurring maintenance windows were scheduled, most patching activities were completed successfully, emergency maintenance followed approval/PIR requirements, and most vendor maintenance agreements were active.

However, CC7.1 is **not fully met / not fully operating effectively** due to several control exceptions across vendor agreement management, vendor SLA escalation, maintenance notification, patch record completeness, and preventive maintenance monitoring.

---

## Findings

| # | Finding Title | Related Section / Evidence | Gap Identified | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | VMware/Broadcom Support Agreement at Risk of Lapse | **POL-MAINT-017 §6.3 Vendor Agreements**; Vendor agreement **VMA-2025-104** | The VMware/Broadcom agreement expires **December 31, 2025**, with renewal negotiations still unresolved due to pricing disputes. The evidence states support will lapse **January 1, 2026** if not renewed. Although the agreement remained active during Q4, there is no evidence of a completed renewal, contingency plan, risk acceptance, or escalation before expiration. This is a gap because hypervisor and vCenter support are foundational to maintaining system components. | **High** |
| 2 | Vendor SLA Breach Not Escalated as Required | **POL-MAINT-017 §6.4 Vendor SLAs**; Vendor agreement **VMA-2025-104** | VMware/Broadcom had one Q4 SLA breach: response to a vCenter patch support ticket took **72 hours** versus a **4-hour SLA**. The breach was documented but explicitly **not escalated to the vendor account manager**, contrary to policy. This weakens vendor accountability and may affect timely maintenance of critical infrastructure. | **Medium** |
| 3 | Patch and Firmware Maintenance Records Are Incomplete / Not Fully Traceable | **POL-MAINT-017 §§4.1, 4.2 Patching; §7.1 Monthly Reports**; Maintenance log and patching records | The maintenance log includes activities that are not reflected in the patching records, including the **November 5 network switch firmware update, CHG-2025-9028**, and the **December 20 additional year-end patching window, CHG-2025-9058**. The patch records also do not include release dates, due dates, patch aging, inventory coverage, or compliance rates by severity. As a result, the auditor cannot fully validate whether all applicable application, database, network, and OS components were patched within policy SLAs. | **Medium** |
| 4 | Planned Maintenance Notification Requirement Not Met | **POL-MAINT-017 §3.1 Standard Windows**; Maintenance window **MW-Q4-012** | The December 20 production maintenance window was planned on December 19 and executed with only **24-hour Slack notice**. Policy requires **48-hour advance notice** for MW-PROD windows, and the email notification to the ops-all distribution list was not sent. While no stakeholder objections were noted, the control did not operate as designed. | **Low** |
| 5 | SSL Certificate Expired Due to Preventive Maintenance / Monitoring Gap | **POL-MAINT-017 §1 Purpose, §2 Scope, §3.2 Emergency Maintenance**; Maintenance window **MW-Q4-011** | The SSL certificate for **api.apex.io** expired on December 18, requiring emergency maintenance. The PIR identified the root cause as a monitoring gap: alerts were configured for 30-day expiry warnings but did not account for 90-day certificates. Although emergency approval, retroactive change, and PIR requirements were met, the certificate was not proactively maintained, causing an avoidable production-impacting event. | **Medium** |
| 6 | Equipment Maintenance Evidence Does Not Fully Demonstrate Required Frequency | **POL-MAINT-017 §5.1 Equipment Maintenance**; Q4 maintenance window log | Policy requires **monthly HVAC maintenance** and quarterly maintenance for UPS, generators, and PDUs. The Q4 log shows HVAC maintenance in November and December, but no clearly documented October HVAC maintenance. Fire suppression maintenance is also required semi-annually, but no schedule or status evidence was provided to confirm whether it was current during Q4. This limits assurance that physical infrastructure components were maintained as required. | **Medium** |
| 7 | Required Maintenance Reporting and Quarterly Review Not Evidenced | **POL-MAINT-017 §§7.1 Monthly Reports, 7.2 Quarterly Reviews** | The policy requires monthly maintenance reports covering maintenance activities, patch compliance, equipment compliance, overdue items, and vendor SLA compliance, as well as quarterly program reviews by the IT Director. No monthly maintenance reports or Q4 review evidence were provided. This is significant because several Q4 issues — vendor SLA breach, expiring VMware agreement, certificate monitoring failure, and missed notification — should have been captured, trended, and escalated through program reporting. | **Medium** |

---

## Conclusion

CC7.1 is **partially satisfied**, but the control is **not operating fully effectively** for Q4 2025.

The strongest evidence supports routine OS patching and scheduled maintenance execution. However, the unresolved VMware/Broadcom support renewal, un-escalated vendor SLA breach, incomplete patch traceability, certificate maintenance failure, and missing program-level reporting indicate gaps in the overall maintenance governance process.
